headline: Functionland Holds Steady as DePIN Storage Sector Matures
subtitle: With 900 active nodes and a consumer-first product suite, Fula network is quietly building for a DePIN market analysts project could reach $3.5 trillion by 2028.
briefing:
Functionland enters mid-2026 without new announcements but with a product foundation increasingly well-positioned for the moment. The Fula network's 900 active nodes underpin a suite of privacy-first consumer tools — encrypted folder collaboration, on-device OCR, and gasless NFT minting — all designed to make decentralized infrastructure invisible to ordinary users. That approach matters more now than ever.

<cite index="1-7">By early 2026, DePIN's combined market capitalization sits in the $9–10 billion range, larger than the oracles sector, while generating tens of millions in monthly on-chain revenue from actual services people pay for.</cite> The sector's center of gravity has shifted decisively toward utility: <cite index="3-8">the dominant question in 2026 is what converts activity into revenue.</cite> Functionland's consumer-facing stack — built on the premise that <cite index="11-2">FxFiles puts photos, docs, and data on the decentralized Fula network with no Web3 knowledge needed</cite> — is a direct answer to that demand-side challenge.

<cite index="6-14">Some estimates suggest the sector could reach a potential valuation of $3.5 trillion by 2028, driven by demand for physical infrastructure to support artificial intelligence and robotics.</cite> Functionland's roadmap aligns with that trajectory: <cite index="18-1">in Q2 2026, the team plans to focus on migrating larger companies and organizations' data from the traditional cloud to the Fula ecosystem.</cite> That enterprise pivot, combined with <cite index="11-32">pricing positioned at up to 2x cheaper than centralized providers and up to 10x cheaper than decentralized alternatives,</cite> could give Fula a credible cost argument for organizations already under cloud spending pressure. Watch for announcements around enterprise onboarding and node growth as the clearest near-term signals of momentum.
